microsoftSourceClaims.claimText: The SharePoint and OneDrive driveItem: extractSensitivityLabels action is generally available in Microsoft Graph v1.0, takes Files.Read.All as the least-privileged permission, and returns an extractSensitivityLabelsResult object, so the auditor can read assigned labels without changing tenant state.

The driveItem: assignSensitivityLabel action is generally available in Microsoft Graph v1.0 but is a protected and metered API that needs Files.ReadWrite.All (or Sites.ReadWrite.All), returns 202 Accepted with a Location long-running operation, and is documented only for the Global service, so the lab keeps bulk assignment out of scope for the first read-only cycle.

Microsoft Graph documents assignSensitivityLabel as a metered API that requires an associated Azure subscription and metered API enablement before use, and states that charges may apply, which supports the cautious cost-planning language used for bulk remediation.

Organization sensitivity label definition enumeration through the security namespace endpoint /security/informationProtection/sensitivityLabels is only available in Microsoft Graph beta and uses InformationProtectionPolicy.Read (delegated) or InformationProtectionPolicy.Read.All (application), so tier mapping should follow tenant beta API governance.

Microsoft Purview service-side auto-labeling for files at rest supports labeling up to 100,000 files per day for the organization and can override an existing lower-priority label, so large tenants need staged remediation waves and remediation manifests should account for override behavior.

Microsoft Purview is replacing parent labels with label groups; migration is irreversible, is automatic for qualifying new tenants, and otherwise rolls out gradually through a portal banner, so the taxonomy snapshot should record label group membership once a tenant migrates.

licenses: Microsoft 365 E5 or A5, Microsoft Purview Suite, or Microsoft 365 Information Protection and Governance licensing assigned in the lab tenant
roles: Sensitivity Label Reader for read-only label review
Information Protection Readers role group in the Microsoft Purview portal
Global Reader for read-only tenant verification
features: Sensitivity labels published from the Microsoft Purview portal
Sensitivity labels enabled for Office files in SharePoint and OneDrive
capacityRequirements: One disposable lab workspace that holds representative sample content only
dataRequirements: Representative sample data only; no production, customer, or regulated records
prerequisites.notes: First lab cycle is read-only and detect-only. No sensitivity label is assigned, removed, or overwritten in the tenant; assignment and removal remain documented-only through the metered assignSensitivityLabel API.

steps.intent: Confirm the FSI sensitivity label taxonomy is published in the Microsoft Purview portal before the audit runs.
steps.expected: Published sensitivity labels (Public through Restricted, or their label group equivalents) are visible on the Sensitivity labels page.
steps.locator: Microsoft Purview portal > Solutions > Information Protection > Sensitivity labels

readBack.expectation: The label list reflects the expected published taxonomy or its migrated label groups.

steps.intent: Confirm the lab operator holds only read-only label roles so the run cannot mutate tenant state.
steps.expected: Operator is assigned Sensitivity Label Reader or Information Protection Readers, and no label write role is active for the run.
steps.operatorNote: Record the assigned read-only roles. Do not proceed if any label write role (for example Sensitivity Label Administrator) is active for this operator.

readBack.expectation: Attestation lists only read-only label roles for the operator.

steps.intent: Run the coverage monitor in documentation-first mode against representative sample data.
steps.expected: Monitor-Compliance.ps1 emits workload coverage metrics, gap findings, and a remediation manifest built from representative sample data.

readBack.reference: Monitor-Compliance console output and monitoring artifacts
readBack.expectation: Coverage report, gap findings, and remediation manifest are produced with representative sample values and no tenant calls.

steps.intent: Export the evidence package and confirm SHA-256 companion files are written.
steps.expected: Export-Evidence.ps1 writes the coverage report, gap findings, remediation manifest, evidence package, and one .sha256 companion per JSON file.

readBack.expectation: Evidence package validates and every JSON artifact has a matching .sha256 companion file.

phases.title: Verify portability and Microsoft source currency

steps.intent: Prove the evidence package uses package-relative artifact paths that survive relocation to an archive or WORM store.
steps.expected: Artifact paths in the package are not rooted, and Test-CopilotGovEvidencePackage returns IsValid after the evidence folder is moved.

readBack.reference: Test-CopilotGovEvidencePackage result on the relocated package
readBack.expectation: IsValid is True after relocation and every artifact path is package-relative.

steps.intent: Confirm the extract, assign, metered, and beta label-definition claims still match current Microsoft Learn guidance.
steps.expected: Microsoft Learn confirms extractSensitivityLabels and assignSensitivityLabel v1.0 state, the metered/protected status of assignSensitivityLabel, and the beta status of the security namespace label-definition endpoint.
steps.operatorNote: Open the cited Microsoft Learn pages for extractSensitivityLabels, assignSensitivityLabel, the metered API overview, and the beta security label-definition list, and record any drift as a BLOCKED disposition with the source excerpt.

readBack.expectation: Cited pages remain published and continue to support the documented API, permission, and lifecycle claims.

steps.intent: Attest that the run applied no sensitivity label change to any tenant object.
steps.expected: Operator attests the run was read-only and detect-only, with no label assigned, removed, or overwritten.
steps.operatorNote: Confirm no assignSensitivityLabel call was made and no tenant label state changed during the run.

readBack.expectation: Attestation confirms no tenant label state was changed.

steps.intent: Remove the local sample evidence output so no representative data is retained after the run.
steps.expected: The local lab-output directory holding sample artifacts is deleted; no tenant object is affected.
steps.command: pwsh -Command "if (Test-Path lab-output) { Remove-Item -Recurse -Force lab-output }; if (Test-Path lab-output) { throw 'Failed to remove local lab-output directory.' }"

readBack.expectation: The lab-output directory no longer exists and no tenant state was touched.

requiredArtifacts.description: PowerShell transcript and console output from Monitor-Compliance.ps1 and Export-Evidence.ps1 in documentation-first mode.
Exported evidence package with package-relative artifact paths and the relocation validation result.
Microsoft Learn citations that confirm the Graph API, metered, licensing, and label-group claims remain current.
